title: Markdown Table Generator
description: Create Markdown tables visually. Edit cells, add or remove rows and columns, set column alignment, and copy the result instantly.

howToUse.label: Edit the table
howToUse.description: Click any cell and type your content. The first row is the header row.

howToUse.label: Adjust structure and alignment
howToUse.description: Use the toolbar to add or remove rows and columns. Click the alignment indicator (L/C/R) under each column header to cycle through left, center, and right alignment.

howToUse.label: Copy the Markdown
howToUse.description: Click "Copy" to copy the generated Markdown table code and paste it into your README, documentation, or any Markdown editor.

faq.question: Where can I use this Markdown table?
faq.answer: Markdown tables are supported in GitHub README files, GitLab, Notion, Obsidian, many documentation tools (Docusaurus, MkDocs), and most modern Markdown editors.

faq.question: What does the L / C / R alignment toggle do?
faq.answer: It controls the alignment marker in the separator row. L adds `:---` (left), C adds `:---:` (center), R adds `---:` (right). Most Markdown renderers respect these alignment hints.

faq.question: Is there a limit on rows or columns?
faq.answer: There is no hard limit, but very wide tables may not display well in narrow viewports. Markdown tables are best kept under 6–8 columns for readability.

faq.question: Can I paste existing table data into the cells?
faq.answer: Each cell accepts free-form text input. For converting structured data to Markdown, consider using the JSON→CSV converter to prepare your data first.
